teams.name: Duke Blue Devils
teams.offenseEfficiency: Rank 1 in Adjusted Offensive Efficiency; most efficient offense in the country. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Consistently dominating defensive efficiency metrics across a massive sample size. Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: 32-2 regular season record; secured the top overall seed.

injuries.detail: Foot fracture — primary ball-handler, critical for perimeter creation and ball security. (9 Pts/4 Reb/3 Ast)
Foot injury — interior depth and rim protection compromised.

keyPlayers.name: Cameron Boozer
keyPlayers.note: National Player of the Year candidate.

keyPlayers.name: Cayden Boozer
keyPlayers.note: Elevated into primary playmaking role with Foster out.

teams.blurb: Duke finished the regular season with a 32-2 record and secured the top overall seed in the East Region. They rank first in both KenPom and BartTorvik. However, their margin for error has evaporated due to foot injuries to point guard Caleb Foster and big man Patrick Ngongba II. Fits the ideal AI Intellipick profile for a deep run.
teams.strengths: Most efficient offense in the country, anchored by generational talent Cameron Boozer.
teams.weaknesses: Depleted backcourt depth and compromised frontcourt rotation due to injuries.

teams.name: Siena Saints
teams.defenseEfficiency: Ranked 15th nationally for scoring defense, holding opponents to 65.7 points per game.
teams.recentMomentum: Won the MAAC Tournament championship.
teams.blurb: The Siena Saints secured a No. 16 seed in the East Region after winning the MAAC Tournament over Merrimack. They face a monumental task against No. 1 Duke.
teams.strengths: Ranked 15th nationally for scoring defense, holding opponents to 65.7 points per game.

teams.name: Ohio State Buckeyes
teams.offenseEfficiency: Highly efficient offense (124.3 AdjO for Thornton).
teams.recentMomentum: Returning to the NCAA Tournament for the first time in three years.

keyPlayers.name: Bruce Thornton
keyPlayers.note: All-time leading scorer in program history.

teams.blurb: Ohio State returns to the NCAA Tournament for the first time in three years under coach Jake Diebler. They are led by all-time leading scorer Bruce Thornton.
teams.strengths: Strong shooting and a highly efficient offense (124.3 AdjO for Thornton).

teams.name: TCU Horned Frogs
teams.defenseEfficiency: Elite defense, boasting a 97.8 Adjusted Defensive Efficiency.
teams.recentMomentum: Won 9 of their last 11 games heading into the tournament.
teams.blurb: TCU enters the tournament as a dangerous 9-seed, having won nine of their last 11 games. They bring a physical, defensive-minded approach from the Big 12.

teams.name: St. John's Red Storm
teams.offenseEfficiency: Hyper-athletic, transition-heavy offensive scheme ranking highly in tempo and fast-break points.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Dominate the possession battle and rank 28th nationally in offensive rebounds.
teams.recentMomentum: Won the Big East regular-season and tournament titles (including a twenty-point demolition of UConn).

keyPlayers.name: Zuby Ejiofor
keyPlayers.note: Big East Player of the Year.

teams.blurb: St. John's is a 5-seed with a 28-6 record. Under Rick Pitino, they absolutely dominate the possession battle and rank 28th nationally in offensive rebounds.
teams.strengths: Relentless on the offensive boards and hyper-athletic, transition-heavy offensive scheme.
teams.weaknesses: Below-average shooting team from both two and three-point range.

teams.name: Northern Iowa Panthers
teams.offenseEfficiency: Dictate a glacial pace (Rank 363 nationally in adjusted tempo); utterly atrocious on the offensive glass (Rank 356 nationally).
teams.defenseEfficiency: Top-25 effective field goal percentage defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Arrives following a grueling, emotional run of four wins in four days to capture the Missouri Valley Conference title.
teams.blurb: Northern Iowa is a 12-seed with a 23-12 record, entering off an emotional MVC title run. They dictate a glacial pace (363rd nationally in adjusted tempo). Characterized by the Slowest Pace metric.
teams.strengths: Massive, switchable rotation contributing to a top-25 effective field goal percentage defense.
teams.weaknesses: Utterly atrocious on the offensive glass, ranking 356th nationally.

teams.name: Kansas Jayhawks

keyPlayers.name: Darryn Peterson
keyPlayers.note: Star freshman guard and potential top NBA pick.

teams.blurb: Kansas is a 4-seed led by star freshman Darryn Peterson. They went 14-8 with him in the lineup and 9-2 without him, but he enters the tournament fully healthy.

teams.name: California Baptist Lancers
teams.blurb: California Baptist is making its first NCAA Tournament appearance in program history, having transitioned to Division I in 2018.

teams.name: Louisville Cardinals
teams.offenseEfficiency: Half-court execution severely compromised without primary playmaker Mikel Brown Jr. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Entered the tournament severely compromised by late-season injuries.

injuries.detail: Back injury — primary playmaker missing. (18 Pts/3 Reb/5 Ast)

teams.blurb: Louisville is a 6-seed with a 23-10 record. They come into the tournament severely compromised by the loss of primary playmaker Mikel Brown Jr. to a back injury. Classified as a Fringe AI Intellipick based on strong overall Net Rating.
teams.strengths: High-major talent and athleticism.
teams.weaknesses: Missing critical playmaking and secondary creation; highly vulnerable to fast-paced opponents.

teams.name: South Florida Bulls
teams.offenseEfficiency: Blistering offensive pace focused heavily on transition three-pointers.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Rank 2 nationally in rebounds (43 per game), ending opponent possessions consistently.
teams.recentMomentum: Riding a massive wave of momentum fueled by an eleven-game winning streak.

keyPlayers.name: Wes Enis
keyPlayers.note: Elite perimeter shooting; recorded eight games with 5+ made threes.

teams.blurb: South Florida is an 11-seed entering with a 25-8 record on an eleven-game winning streak. They are identified as a Very High confidence upset pick. Characterized by the Fastest Pace metric.
teams.strengths: Elite rebounding (43 per game) and blistering offensive pace focusing on transition three-pointers.

teams.name: Michigan State Spartans
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: Michigan State extended its Big Ten record to 28 consecutive NCAA Tournament appearances under Tom Izzo. Classified as a Fringe AI Intellipick based on elite net rating.

teams.name: North Dakota State Bison
teams.blurb: North Dakota State secured the automatic bid from the Summit League, earning a No. 14 seed.

teams.name: UCLA Bruins
teams.blurb: UCLA enters as a 7-seed. They rely heavily on the production of Tyler Bilodeau and Donovan Dent to carry the offensive load.

teams.name: UCF Knights
teams.blurb: UCF earned an at-large bid despite having the lowest KenPom ranking (54) of any power conference team. They feature an explosive offense.
teams.strengths: High-scoring attack, averaging 81 points per game.

teams.name: UConn Huskies
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: UConn enters as a 2-seed after suffering a 20-point blowout loss to St. John's in the Big East title game. Dan Hurley's squad remains a massive threat. Classified as a Fringe AI Intellipick due to playing a slightly too slow pace for optimal models.
teams.strengths: Dynastic pedigree and extreme roster depth.

teams.name: Furman Paladins
teams.blurb: Furman secured a No. 15 seed via the Southern Conference automatic bid. They previously authored a massive upset as a 13-seed in 2023.

teams.name: Arizona Wildcats
teams.offenseEfficiency: Ranks in the top five nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Ranks in the top five nationally in adjusted defensive efficiency. Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Won the Big 12 Tournament.

keyPlayers.name: Koa Peat
keyPlayers.note: Freshman integrated into veteran starting lineup.

keyPlayers.name: Brayden Burries
keyPlayers.note: Freshman integrated into veteran starting lineup.

teams.blurb: Arizona enters as the overall No. 2 seed after winning both the Big 12 regular-season and tournament titles under Coach of the Year Tommy Lloyd. They opened the year with 23 straight wins. Firmly hits the optimal AI Intellipick profile combining elite pace with elite net efficiency.
teams.strengths: Elite balance; top five nationally in both adjusted offensive and defensive efficiency with physical interior dominance.

teams.name: LIU Sharks
teams.blurb: The LIU Sharks represent the Northeast Conference as a 16-seed, drawing a brutal matchup against Arizona.

teams.name: Villanova Wildcats

injuries.detail: Rotation piece missing. (9 Pts/4 Reb/1 Ast)

teams.blurb: Villanova enters as an 8-seed. Their offense is highly reliant on three-point variance, making them vulnerable.

teams.name: Utah State Aggies
teams.blurb: Utah State enters as a 9-seed with a highly balanced offensive attack.

teams.name: Wisconsin Badgers
teams.recentMomentum: Won a thrilling 91-88 overtime game against Illinois in the Big Ten Tournament.
teams.blurb: Wisconsin arrives as a 5-seed, carrying momentum from a dramatic overtime victory over Illinois in their conference tournament.

teams.name: High Point Panthers
teams.recentMomentum: Enters the tournament on a 14-game winning streak.
teams.blurb: High Point is a dangerous 12-seed, entering the NCAA Tournament riding a massive 14-game winning streak.

teams.name: Arkansas Razorbacks
teams.recentMomentum: Won the SEC Tournament.
teams.blurb: Arkansas claimed the SEC Tournament title over Vanderbilt, securing a 4-seed in John Calipari's second season. They are led by freshman star Darius Acuff Jr.

teams.name: Hawai'i Rainbow Warriors
teams.blurb: Hawai'i enters as a 13-seed and is drawing heavy sharp money to cover the spread against Arkansas.

teams.name: BYU Cougars

injuries.detail: Season-ending ACL injury (was averaging 18.0 PPG). (18 Pts/6 Reb/2 Ast)

teams.blurb: BYU boasts the nation's leading scorer in freshman AJ Dybantsa, but they suffered a devastating blow with the season-ending ACL injury to Richie Saunders.

teams.name: Texas Longhorns
teams.recentMomentum: Defeated NC State 68-66 in the First Four.
teams.blurb: Texas was one of the last teams into the field but survived the First Four in Dayton with a 68-66 win over NC State.

teams.name: Gonzaga Bulldogs
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: Gonzaga secured a No. 3 seed. While perhaps lacking the overpowering dominance of vintage Zag squads, they remain highly dangerous. Matches the ideal AI Intellipick profile thanks to top-tier Offense and Defense metrics combined with appropriate pacing.

teams.name: Kennesaw State Owls
teams.blurb: Kennesaw State secured the C-USA automatic bid and is coached by former Alabama player Antoine Pettway.

teams.name: Miami Hurricanes
teams.recentMomentum: Reached the ACC tournament semifinals.
teams.blurb: Miami enters as a 7-seed after a strong run to the ACC tournament semifinals.

teams.name: Missouri Tigers
teams.blurb: Missouri dropped to the 10-line after struggling down the stretch. Historically, the program has never advanced past the Sweet 16.

teams.name: Purdue Boilermakers
teams.recentMomentum: Won the Big Ten Tournament.
teams.blurb: Purdue enters at 27-8 after defeating top-seeded Michigan 80-72 to win the Big Ten Tournament, securing a No. 2 seed.

teams.name: Queens Royals
teams.blurb: Queens is making its first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance after winning the Atlantic Sun championship.

teams.name: Michigan Wolverines
teams.offenseEfficiency: Top-tier offensive efficiency metrics matching historical championship thresholds. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Rank 1 overall in adjusted defensive efficiency; allowing only 68 points per game. Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: 31-3 record entering the tournament.

injuries.detail: Torn ACL — backup point guard. (8 Pts/2 Reb/2 Ast)

teams.blurb: Michigan secured a 1-seed in the Midwest with a 31-3 record. They rank first overall in adjusted defensive efficiency, allowing an average of only 68 points per game. Lands securely in the ideal AI Intellipick profile with an elite balance.
teams.strengths: Defensively elite, allowing only 68 points per game, with surgical passing from the point guard position.
teams.weaknesses: A tendency for careless turnovers, exacerbated by the loss of backup guard L.J. Cason.

teams.name: Howard Bison
teams.recentMomentum: Defeated UMBC 86-83 in the First Four.
teams.blurb: Howard advanced out of the First Four in Dayton. They enter with 23 wins, the most in program history.

teams.name: Georgia Bulldogs
teams.blurb: Georgia is an 8-seed that plays at a blazing speed, leading the nation in fast-break points and averaging nearly 90 points per game. Characterized by the Fastest Pace metric.
teams.strengths: Average 89.8 points per game, the most in program history by over 7 points.

teams.name: Saint Louis Billikens
teams.blurb: Saint Louis made the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2019 under coach Josh Schertz.

teams.name: Texas Tech Red Raiders
teams.offenseEfficiency: Predictable, perimeter-reliant offense missing its primary interior threat.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Highly vulnerable inside and missing rim protection.
teams.recentMomentum: Blown out by Iowa State in the Big 12 Tournament.

injuries.detail: Torn ACL — star forward, forces a predictable offense. (22 Pts/11 Reb/2 Ast)

teams.blurb: Texas Tech is a 5-seed with a 22-10 record. They suffered a devastating blow with the loss of star forward JT Toppin to a torn ACL, leaving them highly vulnerable inside.
teams.strengths: Capable of getting hot from beyond the arc.
teams.weaknesses: Predictable, perimeter-reliant offense missing its primary interior threat and rim protector.

teams.name: Akron Zips
teams.offenseEfficiency: Top 15 nationally in both 2PT and 3PT shooting percentage.
teams.recentMomentum: Won the MAC Tournament on a buzzer-beater 3-pointer by Shammah Scott.
teams.blurb: Akron earned their automatic bid in clutch fashion when Shammah Scott hit a step-back 3-pointer with six seconds left to win the MAC title.
teams.strengths: Elite shooting efficiency (Top 15 nationally in 2PT and 3PT %).

teams.name: Alabama Crimson Tide
teams.offenseEfficiency: Dictate a frenetic pace, ranking 4th nationally in tempo; perimeter spacing compromised due to suspension.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Abysmal defensive rebounding (Rank 292 nationally) and highly suspect interior defense.

injuries.detail: Indefinite suspension — severely compromises perimeter spacing.

teams.blurb: Alabama continues Nate Oats' extreme analytics-driven style, leading the nation in three-point attempts, but their perimeter spacing is compromised. Characterized by the Fastest Pace metric.
teams.strengths: Lead the nation in 3-point attempts per game (35.3), shooting 35.8%.
teams.weaknesses: Abysmal defensive rebounding (292nd) and highly suspect interior defense.

teams.name: Hofstra Pride
teams.offenseEfficiency: Intentionally bogs the game down to a glacial pace (Rank 334 nationally in offensive possession length).
teams.defenseEfficiency: Elite at taking away the rim, ranking 3rd nationally in two-point percentage defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Won 12 of their last 13 games, including a buzzer-beating bank shot to beat Towson in the CAA semis.

keyPlayers.name: Silas Sunday
keyPlayers.note: Seven-foot center providing massive frontcourt length.

teams.blurb: Hofstra enters incredibly hot, winning 12 of 13. They intentionally slow games to a glacial pace to dictate terms.
teams.strengths: Massive frontcourt length, elite tempo control, and ranks third nationally in two-point percentage defense.

teams.name: Tennessee Volunteers
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: Tennessee is a physical menace, leading the entire country by grabbing almost 45% of their missed shots.
teams.strengths: Leads the nation in offensive rebounding percentage (44.7%).

teams.name: Miami (OH) RedHawks
teams.blurb: Miami (OH) went undefeated until a loss in the MAC Tournament to UMass. Despite the 31-1 record, they drew an 11-seed due to a very weak schedule.

teams.name: Virginia Cavaliers
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: Virginia secured a No. 3 seed with their trademark pack-line defense and will look to suffocate the opposition. Evaluated as a Fringe AI Intellipick (metrics point to a pace that's slightly too slow).

teams.name: Wright State Raiders
teams.blurb: Wright State represents the Horizon League as a 14-seed.

teams.name: Kentucky Wildcats
teams.offenseEfficiency: Complete inability to score efficiently in half-court sets; forced into inefficient isolation basketball. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.blurb: Kentucky enters the tournament struggling to score in half-court sets. These issues are heavily exacerbated by the loss of point guard Jaland Lowe to a shoulder injury. Listed as a Top 40 Offense & Fringe Top 25 Defense.
teams.strengths: SEC athleticism.
teams.weaknesses: Poor half-court offense, highly vulnerable to three-point shooting teams, and missing their primary floor general.

teams.name: Santa Clara Broncos
teams.offenseEfficiency: Features a top-25 offensive efficiency rating and massive physical advantage.
teams.blurb: Santa Clara is dancing for the first time in 30 years—since Steve Nash was their point guard. They are well-positioned for an upset.
teams.strengths: Solid structural play and half-court execution.

teams.name: Iowa State Cyclones
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Elite defensive unit (3rd in turnover rate). Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Blew out Texas Tech in the Big 12 Tournament.
teams.blurb: Iowa State is an elite defensive unit (3rd in turnover rate) and enters following a blowout win in their conference tournament. Matches perfectly with the AI Intellipick criteria for ideal pace combined with Top Offense & Defense metrics.

teams.name: Tennessee State Tigers
teams.blurb: Tennessee State represents the Ohio Valley Conference as a 15-seed.

teams.name: Florida Gators
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.recentMomentum: Won the SEC regular season title.
teams.blurb: Florida is the defending national champion and secured a 1-seed for the second straight year after winning the SEC regular-season title. Viewed as a Fringe AI Intellipick because advanced models indicate they play a bit too fast.

teams.name: Prairie View A&M Panthers
teams.recentMomentum: Defeated Lehigh 67-55 in the First Four.
teams.blurb: Prairie View A&M advanced out of the First Four by taking down Lehigh and now faces the defending champs.

teams.name: Clemson Tigers

injuries.detail: Season-ending ACL injury (second-leading scorer). (10 Pts/5 Reb/1 Ast)

teams.blurb: Clemson is an 8-seed but suffered a brutal blow when second-leading scorer Carter Welling was lost to a season-ending ACL injury.

teams.name: Iowa Hawkeyes
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.blurb: Iowa arrives as a 9-seed, preparing for a stylistic rock-fight against Clemson. Characterized by the Slowest Pace metric and listed as a Top 40 Offense & Fringe Top 25 Defense.

teams.name: Vanderbilt Commodores
teams.offenseEfficiency: Top-fifteen adjusted efficiency offense; elite 79% free-throw shooting. Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.recentMomentum: Reached the SEC Tournament title game before falling to Arkansas.
teams.blurb: Vanderbilt is severely underseeded at a 5-seed, arriving with momentum after a run to the SEC title game. Classified as a Fringe AI Intellipick based on Net Rating.
teams.strengths: Elite free-throw shooting (79%), imposing physical forwards, and top-fifteen adjusted efficiency offense.

teams.name: McNeese Cowboys
teams.blurb: The McNeese Cowboys secured a No. 12 seed in the South Region.

teams.name: Nebraska Cornhuskers
teams.recentMomentum: Lost by 16 to Purdue.
teams.blurb: Nebraska is searching for the first NCAA Tournament victory in program history but enters reeling after a 16-point loss to Purdue.

teams.name: Troy Trojans
teams.blurb: Troy qualified for the tournament for the second consecutive year, looking for their first-ever tournament win.

teams.name: North Carolina Tar Heels
teams.offenseEfficiency: Offensive efficiency metrics drop drastically without Caleb Wilson.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Compromised interior defense and traditional rebounding dominance is shifted.

injuries.detail: Season-ending hand fracture — interior enforcer. (9 Pts/5 Reb/2 Ast)

keyPlayers.name: Henri Veesaar
keyPlayers.note: Forced to shoulder an unsustainable rebounding burden.

teams.blurb: North Carolina has one of the best wins of any team, handing Duke one of its two losses, but the loss of Caleb Wilson severely limits their ceiling.
teams.strengths: Traditional blue-blood pedigree.
teams.weaknesses: Compromised interior defense, traditional rebounding dominance is shifted, and frontcourt depth is missing without Caleb Wilson.

teams.name: VCU Rams
teams.offenseEfficiency: High-volume three-point shooting attack.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Relentless ball pressure designed to disrupt half-court sets and force live-ball turnovers.
teams.recentMomentum: Entering as the Atlantic-10 champions, having won 16 of their last 17 games.
teams.blurb: VCU is an 11-seed with a 27-7 record, having won 16 of their last 17 games to win the A-10. Advanced models give them a 53.4% win probability against UNC.
teams.strengths: Relentless ball pressure designed to disrupt half-court sets and force live-ball turnovers, paired with a high-volume three-point shooting attack.

teams.name: Illinois Fighting Illini
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.recentMomentum: Blew a 15-point lead and lost in overtime to Wisconsin in the Big Ten Tournament.
teams.blurb: Illinois possesses a highly productive attack but enters the tournament stinging from a blown 15-point lead in an overtime loss to Wisconsin. Viewed as a Fringe AI Intellipick with a slightly too slow pace.

teams.name: Penn Quakers
teams.recentMomentum: Won the Ivy League tournament in an overtime thriller.
teams.blurb: Penn arrives hot off an overtime thriller to win the Ivy League tournament under first-year coach Fran McCaffery.

teams.name: Saint Mary's Gaels
teams.defenseEfficiency: Play a slow, defensive slog. Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: Coach Randy Bennett has led the Gaels to 11 NCAA appearances over 25 years, but has never won a Sweet 16 game. They play a slow, defensive slog. Viewed as a Fringe AI Intellipick due to their heavily slowed pace.

teams.name: Texas A&M Aggies
teams.offenseEfficiency: Pushes a fast track meet.
teams.blurb: Texas A&M pushes a fast track meet, setting up a massive stylistic clash against Saint Mary's.

teams.name: Houston Cougars
teams.offenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 40 Offense.
teams.defenseEfficiency: Qualifies as a Top 25 Defense.
teams.blurb: This is the first time Houston hasn't earned a 1-seed since 2022. They are led by freshman Kingston Flemings and placed in the South region. Characterized by the Slowest Pace metric.

teams.name: Idaho Vandals
teams.blurb: Idaho is making its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 1990.
